code.value:
from collections.abc import Generator
from typing import Any

import orjson
from fastapi.encoders import jsonable_encoder

# --- FIXED IMPORT: ChatComponent comes from base.io.chat ---
from langflow.base.io.chat import ChatComponent 
from langflow.helpers.data import safe_convert
from langflow.io import BoolInput, HandleInput, MessageTextInput, Output
from langflow.schema.data import Data
from langflow.schema.dataframe import DataFrame
from langflow.schema.message import Message
from langflow.schema.properties import Source
from langflow.utils.constants import MESSAGE_SENDER_AI

class ChatOutput(ChatComponent):
    display_name = "Chat Output"
    description = "A stateless output node that streams the final response back to the REST API payload."
    icon = "MessagesSquare"
    name = "ChatOutput"
    minimized = False # Expanded by default

    inputs = [
        HandleInput(
            name="input_value",
            display_name="Inputs",
            info="Message, Text, or Data to be passed as output.",
            input_types=["Data", "DataFrame", "Message"],
            required=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="session_id",
            display_name="Session ID",
            info="The session ID of the chat. If empty, the current session ID parameter will be used.",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="context_id",
            display_name="Context ID",
            info="The context ID of the chat. Adds an extra layer to the local memory tracking if needed by the frontend.",
            value="",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="sender_name",
            display_name="Sender Name",
            info="Name of the sender.",
            value="Assistant", # Defaulted to Assistant
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="data_template",
            display_name="Data Template",
            value="{text}",
            advanced=True,
            info="Template to convert Data to Text. If left empty, it will be dynamically set to the Data's text key.",
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="clean_data",
            display_name="Basic Clean Data",
            value=True,
            advanced=True,
            info="Whether to strip null/empty values before converting structured Data objects to strings.",
        ),
    ]
    
    outputs = [
        Output(
            display_name="Output Message",
            name="message",
            method="message_response",
        ),
    ]

    def _build_source(self, id_: str | None, display_name: str | None, source: str | None) -> Source:
        source_dict = {}
        if id_: source_dict["id"] = id_
        if display_name: source_dict["display_name"] = display_name
        if source:
            if hasattr(source, "model_name"):
                source_dict["source"] = source.model_name
            elif hasattr(source, "model"):
                source_dict["source"] = str(source.model)
            else:
                source_dict["source"] = str(source)
        return Source(**source_dict)

    async def message_response(self) -> Message:
        # 1. Convert the input to string if needed
        text = self.convert_to_string()

        # 2. Get source properties
        source, _, display_name, source_id = self.get_properties_from_source_component()

        # 3. Create or use existing Message object
        if isinstance(self.input_value, Message) and not self.is_connected_to_chat_input():
            message = self.input_value
            message.text = text
            existing_session_id = message.session_id
        else:
            message = Message(text=text)
            existing_session_id = None

        # 4. Set strict stateless message properties
        message.sender = MESSAGE_SENDER_AI # Hardcoded to Machine
        message.sender_name = self.sender_name
        
        message.session_id = (
            self.session_id or existing_session_id or (self.graph.session_id if hasattr(self, "graph") else None) or ""
        )
        message.context_id = self.context_id
        message.flow_id = self.graph.flow_id if hasattr(self, "graph") else None
        message.properties.source = self._build_source(source_id, display_name, source)

        # 5. FIX: Broadcast the message to the Playground UI!
        # Because this is a custom component, the UI won't automatically draw the chat bubble.
        # We must manually trigger the websocket event so the user sees the output.
        await self.send_message(message)

        self.status = message
        return message

    def _serialize_data(self, data: Data) -> str:
        """Serialize Data object to JSON string."""
        serializable_data = jsonable_encoder(data.data)
        json_bytes = orjson.dumps(serializable_data, option=orjson.OPT_INDENT_2)
        return "```json\n" + json_bytes.decode("utf-8") + "\n```"

    def _validate_input(self) -> None:
        """Validate the input data and raise ValueError if invalid."""
        if self.input_value is None:
            raise ValueError("Input data cannot be None")
            
        if isinstance(self.input_value, list) and not all(
            isinstance(item, Message | Data | DataFrame | str) for item in self.input_value
        ):
            invalid_types = [
                type(item).__name__
                for item in self.input_value
                if not isinstance(item, Message | Data | DataFrame | str)
            ]
            raise TypeError(f"Expected Data or DataFrame or Message or str, got {invalid_types}")
            
        if not isinstance(
            self.input_value,
            Message | Data | DataFrame | str | list | Generator | type(None),
        ):
            type_name = type(self.input_value).__name__
            raise TypeError(f"Expected Data or DataFrame or Message or str, Generator or None, got {type_name}")

    def convert_to_string(self) -> str | Generator[Any, None, None]:
        """Convert input data to string with proper error handling."""
        self._validate_input()
        if isinstance(self.input_value, list):
            clean_data: bool = getattr(self, "clean_data", False)
            return "\n".join([safe_convert(item, clean_data=clean_data) for item in self.input_value])
        if isinstance(self.input_value, Generator):
            return self.input_value
        return safe_convert(self.input_value)

node.description: Executes a hardcoded test request to verify the language model connection.

code.name: code
code.value:
import traceback
from lfx.custom import Component
from lfx.io import Output, HandleInput
from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage
from langchain_core.language_models.chat_models import BaseChatModel
from lfx.schema import Message

from pprint import pprint

class TestInferenceComponent(Component):
    display_name = "Test Inference"
    description = "Executes a hardcoded test request to verify the language model connection."
    icon = "stethoscope"
    category = "testing"

    inputs = [
        HandleInput(
            name="llm",
            display_name="Language Model",
            info="Connect your local model provider here to test the connection.",
            input_types=["LanguageModel", "BaseChatModel"],
            required=True,
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="enable",
            display_name="Enable",
            input_types=["bool"],
            advanced=True,
            info="Whether to run this test",
            value=True,
        ),
    ]

    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Test Result", name="output_message", method="run_test")
    ]

    def run_test(self) -> Message:
        if not self.enable:
            return Message(text=f"Test skipped")
            
        print("\n" + "="*50)
        print("🧪 RUNNING HARDCODED MODEL TEST")
        print("="*50)
        
        if not getattr(self, "llm", None):
            msg = "❌ No Language Model connected!"
            print(msg)
            return Message(text=msg)

        # The hardcoded test message
        test_content = "Hello! This is an API test. Please reply ONLY with the exact phrase: 'Test successful'."
        messages = [HumanMessage(content=test_content)]
        
        print("Sending hardcoded test message...")
        
        kwargs = self.llm._serialized.get('kwargs', {})
        print(f'  Model : {kwargs.get("model_name", None)}')
        print(f'  Base URL : {kwargs.get("openai_api_base", None)}')
        print(f"  Prompt: '{test_content}'\n")
        
        try:
            # We use invoke instead of stream for a simple ping test
            response = self.llm.invoke(messages)
            
            print("✅ SUCCESS! Response Received:")
            print(f'"{response.content}"')
            print("="*50 + "\n")
            
            return Message(text=f"✅ Test Passed!\n\nModel Response:\n{response.content}")
            
        except Exception as e:
            print("❌ FAILED! Connection or API Error:")
            print(str(e))
            print("\n--- Full Error Traceback ---")
            traceback.print_exc()
            print("="*50 + "\n")
            
            # Return the error to the Langflow UI as well
            return Message(text=f"❌ Test Failed!\n\nCheck the console for the full traceback.\n\nError Summary:\n{str(e)}")

from langflow.custom import Component
from langflow.io import HandleInput, Output, DropdownInput

class ModelConfig(Component):
    display_name = "Model Config"
    description = "Extracts the configuration dictionary for a specific semantic model role."
    icon = "Router"
    name = "ModelConfig"

    inputs = [
        HandleInput(
            name="model_configs_dict", 
            display_name="Model Configs",
            input_types=["dict"],
            info="Connect the wire from ChatInput's 'Model Configs' output here."
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="role",
            display_name="Role",
            options=["general", "fast"], # TODO Make this dynamic
            value="general",
            real_time_refresh=True,
        )
    ]

    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Role Config (Dict)", name="role_config_out", method="get_role_config", types=["dict"])
    ]

    def get_role_config(self) -> dict:
        configs = self.model_configs_dict if isinstance(self.model_configs_dict, dict) else {}
        selected_role = getattr(self, "role", "general")
        return configs.get(selected_role, {})

model_configs_dict.info: Connect the wire from ChatInput's 'Model Configs' output here.

import os
import re
import requests
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.language_models.chat_models import BaseChatModel
from typing import Any

from lfx.base.models.model import LCModelComponent
from lfx.io import Output
from lfx.inputs.inputs import DropdownInput, StrInput

# Internal dictionary mapping providers to API spec types
PROVIDER_API_MAP = {
    "vLLM": "openai_compatible",
    "GenAI.mil": "openai_compatible",
    "OpenAI Compatible API": "openai_compatible",
}

DEFAULT_PROVIDER_MODELS = {
    "GenAI.mil": "gemini-3-flash-preview",
}

class LanguageModelComponent(LCModelComponent):
    display_name = "Language Model"
    description = "Configures a local language model to be consumed by inference nodes."
    icon = "brain-circuit"
    category = "models"

    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Language Model", name="language_model", method="build_model")
    ]

    inputs = [
        HandleInput(
            name="config",
            display_name="Config",
            info="Connect a Model Config dict here for stateless override",
            input_types=["dict"],
            required=False,
            advanced=False,
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="provider",
            display_name="Provider",
            info="Select the model provider.",
            options=list(PROVIDER_API_MAP.keys()),
            value="GenAI.mil",
            real_time_refresh=True,
            required=True,
        ),
        StrInput(
            name="base_url",
            display_name="Base URL",
            info="URL of the API Endpoint (e.g., http://localhost:8000)",
            value="https://api.genai.mil",
            real_time_refresh=True,
            required=False,
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="model",
            display_name="Model",
            info="Select your model from the available local instances.",
            options=[],  
            real_time_refresh=True,
            required=False,
        ),
        StrInput(
            name="api_key_file",
            display_name="API Key File",
            info="Path to the file containing the API key.",
            value="~/.secrets/GENAI_MIL_API_KEY",
            required=False,
            real_time_refresh=True,
        ),
    ]

    def _resolve_api_spec(self, provider_name: str) -> str:
        if not provider_name:
            return "openai_compatible"
        clean_name = str(provider_name).strip().lower()
        for k,v in PROVIDER_API_MAP.items():
            if k.strip().lower() == clean_name:
                return v
        return "openai_compatible"

    def _get_clean_urls(self, provider_name: str, base_url: str):
        # Enforce GenAI.mil base URL at runtime regardless of the UI or wire value
        if provider_name == "GenAI.mil":
            raw_url = "https://api.genai.mil"
        else:
            # Fallback to localhost if base_url is completely empty
            raw_url = base_url.rstrip("/") if base_url else "http://localhost:8000"
            
        if raw_url.endswith("/v1"):
            return raw_url[:-3], raw_url
        return raw_url, f"{raw_url}/v1"

    def _get_env_var_name(self, provider_name: str) -> str:
        """
        Converts a provider name to an environment variable format.
        Example: 'GenAI.mil' -> 'GENAI_MIL_API_KEY'
        """
        if not provider_name:
            return "DUMMY_API_KEY"
        
        # Replace any non-alphanumeric character with an underscore, then uppercase
        clean_name = re.sub(r'[^a-zA-Z0-9]', '_', provider_name).upper()
        
        # Consolidate multiple consecutive underscores to a single underscore
        clean_name = re.sub(r'_+', '_', clean_name).strip('_')
        
        return f"{clean_name}_API_KEY"

    def _read_key_from_file(self, file_path: str) -> str:
        """Statelessly reads the API key from the provided file path."""
        if not file_path:
            return ""
        try:
            # Safely expand ~ to the actual home directory
            expanded_path = os.path.expanduser(file_path)
            if os.path.exists(expanded_path):
                with open(expanded_path, "r") as f:
                    return f.read().strip()
            return ""
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error reading API key from {file_path}: {e}")
            return ""

    def update_build_config(self, build_config: dict, field_value: Any, field_name: str | None = None):
        build_config = super().update_build_config(build_config, field_value, field_name)
        
        # Safely extract the provider value from UI
        provider_val = build_config.get("provider", {}).get("value")
        provider_input = provider_val if isinstance(provider_val, str) else "GenAI.mil"

        # Base URL UI Logic
        if "base_url" in build_config:
            if provider_input == "GenAI.mil":
                if isinstance(build_config["base_url"].get("value"), str):
                    build_config["base_url"]["value"] = "https://api.genai.mil"
                build_config["base_url"]["disabled"] = True
            else:
                build_config["base_url"]["disabled"] = False
                current_base = build_config["base_url"].get("value")
                if isinstance(current_base, str) and current_base == "https://api.genai.mil":
                    build_config["base_url"]["value"] = "http://localhost:8000"

        # Auto-Update API Key File Path on Provider Change
        if field_name == "provider" and "api_key_file" in build_config:
            current_api_key = build_config["api_key_file"].get("value")
            if isinstance(current_api_key, str) or current_api_key is None:
                env_var_name = self._get_env_var_name(provider_input)
                build_config["api_key_file"]["value"] = f"~/.secrets/{env_var_name}"

        # Fetch Models for Standalone Dropdown
        # Detect if the target fields are directly wired (wires appear as dicts in build_config)
        is_model_wired = isinstance(build_config.get("model", {}).get("value"), dict)
        is_provider_wired = isinstance(build_config.get("provider", {}).get("value"), dict)
        
        if not (is_model_wired or is_provider_wired):
            api_spec = self._resolve_api_spec(provider_input)
            
            if api_spec == "openai_compatible":
                current_url = build_config.get("base_url", {}).get("value")
                if not isinstance(current_url, str):
                    current_url = "https://api.genai.mil" if provider_input == "GenAI.mil" else "http://localhost:8000"
                    
                try:
                    check_host = current_url.rstrip("/")
                    if check_host.endswith("/v1"):
                        check_host = check_host[:-3]
                    
                    raw_ui_path = build_config.get("api_key_file", {}).get("value")
                    raw_ui_path = raw_ui_path if isinstance(raw_ui_path, str) else ""
                    actual_key = self._read_key_from_file(raw_ui_path)
                    
                    headers = {"Content-Type": "application/json"}
                    if actual_key:
                        headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {actual_key}"

                    response = requests.get(f"{check_host}/v1/models", headers=headers, timeout=5)
                    
                    if response.status_code == 200:
                        data = response.json()
                        models = [m["id"] for m in data.get("data", [])]
                        
                        if models:
                            build_config["model"]["options"] = models
                            current_model = build_config.get("model", {}).get("value")
                            preferred_default = DEFAULT_PROVIDER_MODELS.get(provider_input)
                            
                            if current_model not in models or field_name == "provider":
                                if preferred_default and preferred_default in models:
                                    build_config["model"]["value"] = preferred_default
                                else:
                                    build_config["model"]["value"] = models[0]
                        else:
                            # Fallback if the server responds but returns an empty list
                            preferred_default = DEFAULT_PROVIDER_MODELS.get(provider_input, "gpt-3.5-turbo")
                            build_config["model"]["options"] = [preferred_default]
                            build_config["model"]["value"] = preferred_default
                    else:
                        # Fallback for HTTP errors (e.g., 401 Unauthorized at design time)
                        preferred_default = DEFAULT_PROVIDER_MODELS.get(provider_input, "gpt-3.5-turbo")
                        build_config["model"]["options"] = [preferred_default]
                        if not build_config.get("model", {}).get("value"):
                            build_config["model"]["value"] = preferred_default
                except Exception:
                    # Fallback for Network/Timeout errors.
                    # We use the offline default model instead of a breaking error string!
                    preferred_default = DEFAULT_PROVIDER_MODELS.get(provider_input, "gpt-3.5-turbo")
                    build_config["model"]["options"] = [preferred_default]
                    build_config["model"]["value"] = preferred_default
        else:
            # If directly wired, keep options clear to avoid dropdown validation constraints
            build_config["model"]["options"] = []

        return build_config

    def build_model(self) -> BaseChatModel:
        # 1. Start with the Standalone UI defaults
        provider_name = getattr(self, "provider", "GenAI.mil")
        raw_url_input = getattr(self, "base_url", "http://localhost:8000")
        actual_model = getattr(self, "model", "")
        raw_path = getattr(self, "api_key_file", "~/.secrets/GENAI_MIL_API_KEY")

        # 2. Intercept with Dynamic Overrides if a Dict is wired!
        override = getattr(self, "config", {})
        if isinstance(override, dict) and override:
            # Overwrite variables only if the proxy actually provided them
            provider_name = override.get("provider") or provider_name
            raw_url_input = override.get("base_url") or raw_url_input
            actual_model = override.get("model") or actual_model
            api_key_path_override = override.get("api_key_file")
            if api_key_path_override:
                raw_path = api_key_path_override

        # 3. Process the finalized variables
        _pw_host = os.environ.get("PW_PLATFORM_HOST", "").replace("https://", "").rstrip("/")
        if _pw_host:
            provider_name = "OpenAI Compatible API"
            raw_url_input = os.environ.get("PW_OPENAI_BASE_URL") or f"https://{_pw_host}/api/openai/v1"
            actual_model = os.environ.get("PW_TEST_MODEL") or "org:glm/glm-5.1"  # force full id; UI dropdown can mangle org:* ids
        api_spec = self._resolve_api_spec(provider_name)
        
        # Initialize an empty variable to hold the final model
        model = None

        if api_spec == "openai_compatible":
            _, openai_api_base = self._get_clean_urls(provider_name, raw_url_input)
            
            if not actual_model:
                actual_model = DEFAULT_PROVIDER_MODELS.get(provider_name, "gpt-3.5-turbo")
                
            if not raw_path or raw_path == getattr(self, "api_key_file", ""):
                env_var_name = self._get_env_var_name(provider_name)
                raw_path = f"~/.secrets/{env_var_name}"
                
            actual_api_key = self._read_key_from_file(raw_path) or os.environ.get("PW_API_KEY") or "dummy"

            # ACTIVATE platform org models (org:*) need an X-Allocation header.
            _alloc = os.environ.get("PW_ALLOCATION")
            if not _alloc and _pw_host:
                try:
                    _r = requests.get(f"https://{_pw_host}/api/allocations",
                                      headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {actual_api_key}"}, timeout=8)
                    if _r.status_code == 200:
                        _names = [a.get("name", "") for a in _r.json() if a.get("name")]
                        _alloc = next((n for n in _names if "LLM" in n), _names[0] if _names else "")
                except Exception:
                    _alloc = ""
            _extra_headers = {"X-Allocation": _alloc} if _alloc else None

            try:
                model = ChatOpenAI(
                    base_url=openai_api_base,
                    model=actual_model,
                    api_key=actual_api_key,
                    default_headers=_extra_headers,
                    streaming=True, 
                )
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"ChatOpenAI Initialization Failed: {e}")
        else:
            print(f"Unsupported API Spec: {api_spec}")
            raise ValueError(f"No execution logic implemented for API spec: {api_spec}")

        # 4. Bind to status so users see exactly what the proxy injected
        self.status = model
        
        return model

node.description: Create a prompt template with dynamic variables and optional cache-busting.

add_cache_busting_salt.info: Appends a hidden random UUID to force API gateways to treat this as a fresh request, preventing unwanted session memory.

code.name: code
code.value:
import uuid
from typing import Any

from lfx.base.prompts.api_utils import process_prompt_template
from lfx.custom.custom_component.component import Component
from lfx.inputs.input_mixin import FieldTypes
from lfx.inputs.inputs import DefaultPromptField
from lfx.io import BoolInput, MessageTextInput, Output, PromptInput
from lfx.log.logger import logger
from lfx.schema.dotdict import dotdict
from lfx.schema.message import Message
from lfx.template.utils import update_template_values
from lfx.utils.mustache_security import validate_mustache_template

class PromptComponent(Component):
    display_name: str = "Prompt Template"
    description: str = "Create a prompt template with dynamic variables and optional cache-busting."
    documentation: str = "https://docs.langflow.org/components-prompts"
    icon = "prompts"
    trace_type = "prompt"
    name = "Prompt Template"

    inputs = [
        PromptInput(name="template", display_name="Template"),
        BoolInput(
            name="use_double_brackets",
            display_name="Use Double Brackets",
            value=False,
            advanced=True,
            info="Use {{variable}} syntax instead of {variable}.",
            real_time_refresh=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="tool_placeholder",
            display_name="Tool Placeholder",
            tool_mode=True,
            advanced=True,
            info="A placeholder input for tool mode.",
        ),
        # NEW: Cache Busting Toggle
        BoolInput(
            name="add_cache_busting_salt",
            display_name="Add Cache-Busting Salt",
            value=False,
            advanced=True,
            info="Appends a hidden random UUID to force API gateways to treat this as a fresh request, preventing unwanted session memory.",
        ),
    ]

    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Prompt", name="prompt", method="build_prompt"),
    ]

    def update_build_config(self, build_config: dotdict, field_value: Any, field_name: str | None = None) -> dotdict:
        """Update the template field type based on the selected mode."""
        if field_name == "use_double_brackets":
            # Change the template field type based on mode
            is_mustache = field_value is True
            if is_mustache:
                build_config["template"]["type"] = FieldTypes.MUSTACHE_PROMPT.value
            else:
                build_config["template"]["type"] = FieldTypes.PROMPT.value

            # Re-process the template to update variables when mode changes
            template_value = build_config.get("template", {}).get("value", "")
            if template_value:
                # Ensure custom_fields is properly initialized
                if "custom_fields" not in build_config:
                    build_config["custom_fields"] = {}

                # Clean up fields from the OLD mode before processing with NEW mode
                old_custom_fields = build_config["custom_fields"].get("template", [])
                for old_field in list(old_custom_fields):
                    if old_field in old_custom_fields:
                        old_custom_fields.remove(old_field)
                    build_config.pop(old_field, None)

                # Try to process template with new mode
                try:
                    if is_mustache:
                        validate_mustache_template(template_value)

                    _ = process_prompt_template(
                        template=template_value,
                        name="template",
                        custom_fields=build_config["custom_fields"],
                        frontend_node_template=build_config,
                        is_mustache=is_mustache,
                    )
                except ValueError as e:
                    logger.debug(f"Template validation failed during mode switch: {e}")

        return build_config

    async def build_prompt(self) -> Message:
        use_double_brackets = self.use_double_brackets if hasattr(self, "use_double_brackets") else False
        template_format = "mustache" if use_double_brackets else "f-string"

        prompt = await Message.from_template_and_variables(template_format=template_format, **self._attributes)
        
        # NEW: Inject cache-busting salt if the toggle is active
        if getattr(self, "add_cache_busting_salt", False):
            salt = f"\n\n<!-- stateless_req_id: {uuid.uuid4()} -->"
            prompt.text += salt

        self.status = prompt.text
        return prompt

    def _update_template(self, frontend_node: dict):
        prompt_template = frontend_node["template"]["template"]["value"]
        use_double_brackets = frontend_node["template"].get("use_double_brackets", {}).get("value", False)
        is_mustache = use_double_brackets is True

        try:
            if is_mustache:
                validate_mustache_template(prompt_template)

            custom_fields = frontend_node["custom_fields"]
            frontend_node_template = frontend_node["template"]
            
            _ = process_prompt_template(
                template=prompt_template,
                name="template",
                custom_fields=custom_fields,
                frontend_node_template=frontend_node_template,
                is_mustache=is_mustache,
            )
        except ValueError as e:
            logger.debug(f"Template validation failed in _update_template: {e}")

        return frontend_node

    async def update_frontend_node(self, new_frontend_node: dict, current_frontend_node: dict):
        """This function is called after the code validation is done."""
        frontend_node = await super().update_frontend_node(new_frontend_node, current_frontend_node)
        template = frontend_node["template"]["template"]["value"]
        use_double_brackets = frontend_node["template"].get("use_double_brackets", {}).get("value", False)
        is_mustache = use_double_brackets is True

        try:
            if is_mustache:
                validate_mustache_template(template)

            _ = process_prompt_template(
                template=template,
                name="template",
                custom_fields=frontend_node["custom_fields"],
                frontend_node_template=frontend_node["template"],
                is_mustache=is_mustache,
            )
        except ValueError as e:
            logger.debug(f"Template validation failed in update_frontend_node: {e}")

        update_template_values(new_template=frontend_node, previous_template=current_frontend_node["template"])
        return frontend_node

    def _get_fallback_input(self, **kwargs):
        return DefaultPromptField(**kwargs)

code.value:
from lfx.custom import Component
from lfx.field_typing.range_spec import RangeSpec
from lfx.io import Output, MessageInput, MultilineInput, BoolInput, HandleInput, SliderInput, IntInput
from langchain_core.messages import SystemMessage, HumanMessage, AIMessage, BaseMessage
from langchain_core.language_models.chat_models import BaseChatModel
from lfx.schema import Message
from lfx.utils.constants import MESSAGE_SENDER_AI

class LanguageModelInferenceComponent(Component):
    display_name = "Language Model Inference"
    description = "Executes streaming inference using a connected Language Model and optional chat history."
    icon = "message-circle"
    category = "processing"
    name = "LanguageModelInferenceComponent"

    inputs = [
        HandleInput(
            name="llm",
            display_name="Language Model",
            info="Connect your local model provider here.",
            input_types=["LanguageModel", "BaseChatModel"],
            required=True,
        ),
        MessageInput(
            name="input_value",
            display_name="User Prompt",
            info="The current input text to send to the model",
            required=True,
        ),
        MultilineInput(
            name="system_message",
            display_name="System Prompt",
            info="A system message that helps set the behavior of the assistant",
            advanced=False,
        ),
        HandleInput(
            name="chat_history",
            display_name="Chat History",
            info="Connect previous chat messages (up to but not including the current input).",
            input_types=["Message", "list"],
            required=False,
            is_list=True,
        ),
        SliderInput(
            name="temperature",
            display_name="Temperature",
            value=1.0,
            info="Controls randomness in responses",
            input_types=["float"],
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=2, step=0.01),
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="max_tokens",
            display_name="Max Tokens",
            info="Maximum number of tokens to generate. Leave empty (None) for no limit.",
            value=None, 
            input_types=["int"],
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="stream",
            display_name="Stream Output",
            input_types=["bool"],
            info="Whether to stream the text chunk by chunk to the UI.",
            value=True,
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="stop_on_error",
            display_name="Stop on Error",
            input_types=["bool"],
            info="If True, the flow will crash upon an API error. If False, it passes the error forward as a Message.",
            value=True,
            advanced=True,
        ),
    ]

    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Message", name="output_message", method="run_inference")
    ]

    async def run_inference(self) -> Message:
        messages: list[BaseMessage] = []
        
        if getattr(self, "system_message", None):
            messages.append(SystemMessage(content=self.system_message))
            
        if getattr(self, "chat_history", None):
            history_items = self.chat_history if isinstance(self.chat_history, list) else [self.chat_history]
            for msg in history_items:
                if isinstance(msg, Message):
                    text = msg.text
                    sender = getattr(msg, "sender", "User")
                elif isinstance(msg, dict):
                    text = msg.get("text", "")
                    sender = msg.get("sender", "User")
                else:
                    text = str(msg)
                    sender = "User"
                    
                if sender == "AI":
                    messages.append(AIMessage(content=text))
                else:
                    messages.append(HumanMessage(content=text))

        if getattr(self, "input_value", None):
            user_text = self.input_value.text if hasattr(self.input_value, "text") else str(self.input_value)
            messages.append(HumanMessage(content=user_text))
            
        bind_kwargs = {"temperature": getattr(self, "temperature", 1.0)}
        if getattr(self, "max_tokens", None) is not None:
            bind_kwargs["max_tokens"] = self.max_tokens
            
        executable_llm = self.llm.bind(**bind_kwargs)
        
        if hasattr(self, "graph"):
            session_id = self.graph.session_id
        elif hasattr(self, "_session_id"):
            session_id = self._session_id
        else:
            session_id = None
            
        try:
            if getattr(self, "stream", True):
                # Streaming Path
                model_message = Message(
                    text=executable_llm.astream(messages), 
                    sender=MESSAGE_SENDER_AI,
                    sender_name="AI",
                    session_id=session_id,
                    # FIX 2: The "partial" state forces the UI to anchor the message at the bottom of the chat!
                    properties={"icon": self.icon, "state": "partial"} 
                )
                
                lf_message = await self.send_message(model_message)
                return lf_message
                
            else:
                # Non-Streaming Path
                response = await executable_llm.ainvoke(messages)
                model_message = Message(
                    text=str(response.content),
                    sender=MESSAGE_SENDER_AI,
                    sender_name="AI",
                    session_id=session_id,
                    # No "partial" state needed here, because it's already a complete text string.
                    properties={"icon": self.icon}
                )
                
                # FIX 1: Explicitly call send_message here so it renders in the Playground 
                # even when Stream Output is False!
                lf_message = await self.send_message(model_message)
                return lf_message

        except Exception as e:
            # If the user toggled the advanced "Stop on Error" setting to True, crash normally.
            if getattr(self, "stop_on_error", False):
                raise e
            
            # Otherwise, package the exception into a Message and pass it downstream to the Catcher.
            error_message = Message(
                text=str(e),
                sender=MESSAGE_SENDER_AI,
                sender_name="AI",
                session_id=session_id,
                properties={"icon": "alert-triangle"}
            )
            lf_message = await self.send_message(error_message)
            return lf_message

code.value:
from collections.abc import Generator
from typing import Any

import orjson
from fastapi.encoders import jsonable_encoder

# --- FIXED IMPORT: ChatComponent comes from base.io.chat ---
from langflow.base.io.chat import ChatComponent 
from langflow.helpers.data import safe_convert
from langflow.io import BoolInput, HandleInput, MessageTextInput, Output
from langflow.schema.data import Data
from langflow.schema.dataframe import DataFrame
from langflow.schema.message import Message
from langflow.schema.properties import Source
from langflow.utils.constants import MESSAGE_SENDER_AI

class ChatOutput(ChatComponent):
    display_name = "Chat Output"
    description = "A stateless output node that streams the final response back to the REST API payload."
    icon = "MessagesSquare"
    name = "ChatOutput"
    minimized = False # Expanded by default

    inputs = [
        HandleInput(
            name="input_value",
            display_name="Inputs",
            info="Message, Text, or Data to be passed as output.",
            input_types=["Data", "DataFrame", "Message"],
            required=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="session_id",
            display_name="Session ID",
            info="The session ID of the chat. If empty, the current session ID parameter will be used.",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="context_id",
            display_name="Context ID",
            info="The context ID of the chat. Adds an extra layer to the local memory tracking if needed by the frontend.",
            value="",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="sender_name",
            display_name="Sender Name",
            info="Name of the sender.",
            value="Assistant", # Defaulted to Assistant
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="data_template",
            display_name="Data Template",
            value="{text}",
            advanced=True,
            info="Template to convert Data to Text. If left empty, it will be dynamically set to the Data's text key.",
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="clean_data",
            display_name="Basic Clean Data",
            value=True,
            advanced=True,
            info="Whether to strip null/empty values before converting structured Data objects to strings.",
        ),
    ]
    
    outputs = [
        Output(
            display_name="Output Message",
            name="message",
            method="message_response",
        ),
    ]

    def _build_source(self, id_: str | None, display_name: str | None, source: str | None) -> Source:
        source_dict = {}
        if id_: source_dict["id"] = id_
        if display_name: source_dict["display_name"] = display_name
        if source:
            if hasattr(source, "model_name"):
                source_dict["source"] = source.model_name
            elif hasattr(source, "model"):
                source_dict["source"] = str(source.model)
            else:
                source_dict["source"] = str(source)
        return Source(**source_dict)

    async def message_response(self) -> Message:
        # 1. Convert the input to string if needed
        text = self.convert_to_string()

        # 2. Get source properties
        source, _, display_name, source_id = self.get_properties_from_source_component()

        # 3. Create or use existing Message object
        if isinstance(self.input_value, Message) and not self.is_connected_to_chat_input():
            message = self.input_value
            message.text = text
            existing_session_id = message.session_id
        else:
            message = Message(text=text)
            existing_session_id = None

        # 4. Set strict stateless message properties
        message.sender = MESSAGE_SENDER_AI # Hardcoded to Machine
        message.sender_name = self.sender_name
        
        message.session_id = (
            self.session_id or existing_session_id or (self.graph.session_id if hasattr(self, "graph") else None) or ""
        )
        message.context_id = self.context_id
        message.flow_id = self.graph.flow_id if hasattr(self, "graph") else None
        message.properties.source = self._build_source(source_id, display_name, source)

        # 5. FIX: Broadcast the message to the Playground UI!
        # Because this is a custom component, the UI won't automatically draw the chat bubble.
        # We must manually trigger the websocket event so the user sees the output.
        await self.send_message(message)

        self.status = message
        return message

    def _serialize_data(self, data: Data) -> str:
        """Serialize Data object to JSON string."""
        serializable_data = jsonable_encoder(data.data)
        json_bytes = orjson.dumps(serializable_data, option=orjson.OPT_INDENT_2)
        return "```json\n" + json_bytes.decode("utf-8") + "\n```"

    def _validate_input(self) -> None:
        """Validate the input data and raise ValueError if invalid."""
        if self.input_value is None:
            raise ValueError("Input data cannot be None")
            
        if isinstance(self.input_value, list) and not all(
            isinstance(item, Message | Data | DataFrame | str) for item in self.input_value
        ):
            invalid_types = [
                type(item).__name__
                for item in self.input_value
                if not isinstance(item, Message | Data | DataFrame | str)
            ]
            raise TypeError(f"Expected Data or DataFrame or Message or str, got {invalid_types}")
            
        if not isinstance(
            self.input_value,
            Message | Data | DataFrame | str | list | Generator | type(None),
        ):
            type_name = type(self.input_value).__name__
            raise TypeError(f"Expected Data or DataFrame or Message or str, Generator or None, got {type_name}")

    def convert_to_string(self) -> str | Generator[Any, None, None]:
        """Convert input data to string with proper error handling."""
        self._validate_input()
        if isinstance(self.input_value, list):
            clean_data: bool = getattr(self, "clean_data", False)
            return "\n".join([safe_convert(item, clean_data=clean_data) for item in self.input_value])
        if isinstance(self.input_value, Generator):
            return self.input_value
        return safe_convert(self.input_value)

data_template.info: Template to convert Data to Text. If left empty, it will be dynamically set to the Data's text key.
data_template.name: data_template

input_value.info: Message, Text, or Data to be passed as output.
input_value.name: input_value

session_id.name: session_id
